topic.name: Historic Architecture and Urban Development
topic.description: The documents discuss significant historic districts, architectural styles, and urban development in various cities and regions, highlighting their cultural and historical importance.

Document 1: Central Troy Historic District is described as one of the most perfectly preserved 19th-century downtowns.
It has nearly 700 properties in a variety of architectural styles.
Document 4: The architecture of Scotland spans from the Neolithic era to the present day.
It includes various historical building styles and influences.
Document 6: Ludlow Castle is a ruined medieval fortification, significant in the context of English history.
It played an important role during the civil war of the 12th century.

topic.name: Mycena and Boletaceae Fungi
topic.description: The documents discuss various species of fungi, primarily focusing on the Mycena genus and other related families such as Boletaceae, detailing their taxonomy, morphology, and ecological associations.

Document 1: Mycena fuscoaurantiaca is a species of mushroom in the Mycenaceae family.
first collected by Japanese mycologist Haruki Takahashi in 1999
Document 2: Mycena nidificata is a species of fungus in the Mycenaceae family of the Agaricales.
first collected in 2000 and reported as a new species in 2007
Document 3: Boletus rubroflammeus is a species of bolete fungus in the family Boletaceae.
First described from Michigan in 1971
Document 4: Mycena maculata, commonly known as the reddish-spotted Mycena, is a species of fungus in the Mycenaceae family.
The saprobic fungus is found in Europe and North America.
Document 5: Pluteus nevadensis is a species of fungus in the agaric family Pluteaceae.
described as new to science in 2010
Document 6: Pulveroboletus bembae is a species of fungus in the Boletaceae family that was first described in 2009.
the fungus grows in a mycorrhizal relationship with Gilbertiodendron dewevrei
Document 7: Lactifluus deceptivus, commonly known as the deceiving milkcap, is a common species of fungus in the Russulaceae family.
It produces large mushrooms with funnel-shaped caps reaching up to 25 cm in diameter.

topic.name: Music Singles and Releases
topic.description: The documents discuss various music singles, their releases, and critical receptions from different artists across genres.

Document 1: 'Paparazzi' is a song recorded in the Japanese language by South Korean girl group Girls' Generation
The track was described as an electropop and R & B song.
Document 2: 'Gate (Sore wa Akatsuki no you ni)' is a song recorded by Japanese group Kisida Kyoudan & The Akebosi Rockets
Musically, 'Gate' is a rock song that features guitars, drums, and bass guitars in its instrumentation.
Document 3: 'Lollipop (Param Pam Pam)' is the debut song recorded by Romanian recording artist Alexandra Stan
The music video for 'Lollipop' features Stan and other female dancers dancing to the song.
Document 4: 'Give It Up to Me' is a song by Colombian singer-songwriter Shakira featuring American rapper Lil Wayne
Upon its release, the song received generally favorable reviews from music critics.
Document 5: 'Above and Beyoncé' is a remix and video album by American R & B singer Beyoncé
The remix version of 'Ego' included on the album was nominated for Best Rap / Sung Collaboration.
Document 6: 'Here with Me' is a song by Christian rock band, MercyMe
It attained positive critical reception and peaked at No. 1 on multiple Christian radio chart formats.
Document 7: 'Lollipop (Param Pam Pam)' is the debut song recorded by Romanian recording artist Alexandra Stan
Commercially, the single peaked at number 18 on her native Romanian Top 100.

topic.name: Military Aircraft History
topic.description: This topic encompasses the development, roles, and operational history of various military aircraft used by different nations, particularly during the 20th century.

Document 1: The Panavia Tornado is a family of twin-engine, variable-sweep wing multirole combat aircraft.
The Tornado was used by the Royal Air Force (RAF), Italian Air Force and RSAF during the 1991 Gulf War.
Document 2: The United Kingdom operated the McDonnell Douglas F-4 Phantom II as one of its principal combat aircraft from the 1960s to the early 1990s.
The Phantom was procured to serve in both the Fleet Air Arm and Royal Air Force in several roles including air defence, close air support, low-level strike and tactical reconnaissance.
Document 3: RAF Coastal Command was a formation within the Royal Air Force (RAF).
Its primary task was to protect convoys from the German Kriegsmarine's U-boat force.
Document 4: The Heinkel He 111 was a German aircraft designed by Siegfried and Walter Günter at Heinkel Flugzeugwerke in 1934.
It was used in a variety of roles on every front in the European theatre.
Document 5: The Avro Vulcan is a jet-powered tailless delta wing high-altitude strategic bomber, which was operated by the Royal Air Force (RAF) from 1956 until 1984.
The Vulcan was the backbone of the United Kingdom’s airborne nuclear deterrent during much of the Cold War.
Document 6: The Boeing B-52 Stratofortress is a long-range, subsonic, jet-powered strategic bomber.
The B-52 has been in active service with the USAF since 1955.
Document 7: The Junkers Ju 87 or Stuka was a German dive bomber and ground-attack aircraft designed by Hermann Pohlmann.
The Ju 87 made its combat debut in 1937 with the Luftwaffe's Condor Legion during the Spanish Civil War.

topic.name: Cricket History and Players
topic.description: The documents discuss notable cricket teams and players, highlighting their achievements and contributions to the sport.

the team is famous for being the first Test match side to play an entire tour of England without losing a match

described by Wisden as, '... a master of nearly every stroke ...'

6: Irfan Pathan is an Indian cricketer who made his debut for India in the 2003 / 04 Border-Gavaskar Trophy

topic.name: Highway Systems in the U.S.
topic.description: The documents collectively discuss various highways and routes within the U.S. highway system, detailing their lengths, connections, and historical developments.

Planning for the route began in the 1950s
Document 2: Interstate 95 is an Interstate highway running along the East Coast of the United States

Document 3: U.S. Route 15 is a part of the U.S. Highway System that runs from Walterboro, South Carolina to Painted Post, New York
The highway connects the county seat of Frederick with Point of Rocks

I-70 is the primary east-west Interstate in Maryland
Document 5: Maryland Route 2 is the longest state highway in the U.S. state of Maryland
The route runs concurrent with MD 4 through much of Calvert County
Document 6: U.S. Route 13 is a U.S. highway running from Fayetteville, North Carolina north to Morrisville, Pennsylvania
US 13 connects many important cities and towns in Delaware
Document 7: Delaware Route 1 is a 103.02-mile-long state highway in the U.S. state of Delaware
The route runs from the Maryland border in Fenwick Island, Sussex County, where it continues into that state as Maryland Route 528

topic.name: Political Leaders and Elections
topic.description: This topic encompasses influential political figures and their roles in elections and governance in North America.

Document 1: Stephen Harper served as the 22nd Prime Minister of Canada.
He was a founding member of the Reform Party.
Document 2: Al Gore served as the 45th Vice President of the United States.
He was the Democratic Party's nominee for President in 2000.
Document 3: Jennifer Brunner served as the Ohio Secretary of State.
She focused on election law and campaign finance law.
Document 4: Singapore has a multi-party parliamentary system of representative democracy.
Parliamentary elections in Singapore are required to be held regularly.
Document 5: Ted Kennedy was a United States Senator from Massachusetts.
He was a prominent member of the Democratic Party.
Document 6: George W. Bush served as the 43rd President of the United States.
He was elected president in 2000 after a controversial election against Al Gore.
Document 7: Bill Clinton was the 42nd President of the United States.
He was a member of the Democratic Party and previously Governor of Arkansas.

topic.name: Early 20th Century Cruisers
topic.description: The documents discuss various classes of armored cruisers and their roles in naval warfare during the early 20th century, particularly focusing on the German Imperial Navy and its engagements in World War I.

Document 1: The Scharnhorst class was the last traditional class of armored cruisers built by the Kaiserliche Marine.
Both ships had short careers; shortly before the outbreak of World War I, the ships departed the German colony at Tsingtao.
Document 2: SMS Prinz Heinrich was a unique German armored cruiser built at the turn of the 20th century for the Imperial German Navy.
After the outbreak of World War I in August 1914, the ship participated in the operation against the British coast in December 1914.
Document 3: The Minotaur class was a three-ship class of armoured cruisers built in the first decade of the twentieth century for the Royal Navy.
When World War I began in August 1914, Defence participated in the pursuit of the German ships SMS Goeben and SMS Breslau.
Document 4: The Prinz Adalbert class was a type of armored cruiser built for the Imperial German Navy in the early 1900s.
Both vessels were mobilized and assigned to the cruiser squadron in the Baltic.
Document 5: Navarin was a pre-dreadnought battleship built for the Imperial Russian Navy in the late 1880s and early 1890s.
During the Battle of Tsushima in May 1905, she was sunk by Japanese destroyers.
Document 6: HMS Triumph was the second of the two Swiftsure-class pre-dreadnought battleships of the Royal Navy.
Triumph participated in the hunt for the German East Asia Squadron of Maximilian Graf von Spee.
Document 7: The Braunschweig-class battleships were pre-dreadnought battleships of the Kaiserliche Marine.
The five ships all saw active service during World War I, primarily as coast defense ships in the IV Battle Squadron.

topic.name: The Boat Race History
topic.description: The documents discuss the historical annual rowing competition known as The Boat Race between the Universities of Oxford and Cambridge.

Document 1: The Boat Race is a side-by-side rowing race between crews from the Universities of Oxford and Cambridge along the River Thames.
The race was first held in 1829, and since 1845 has taken place on the 4.2-mile (6.8 km) Championship Course on the River Thames.
Document 2: The Boat Race is a side-by-side rowing race between crews from the Universities of Oxford and Cambridge along the River Thames in London.
Cambridge won by ten lengths in a time of 23 minutes 1 second, taking the overall record in the event to 49 – 43 in their favour.
Document 3: The Boat Race is a side-by-side rowing competition between the University of Oxford and the University of Cambridge along the River Thames.
Cambridge won by three-and-a-half lengths in a time of 19 minutes 25 seconds, the fastest winning time for four years.
Document 4: The Boat Race is an annual side-by-side rowing race between crews from the Universities of Oxford and Cambridge along the River Thames.
Oxford won by a margin of seven lengths in a time of 20 minutes 12 seconds, taking the overall record to 21 – 17 in their favour.
Document 5: The Boat Race is a side-by-side rowing race between crews from the Universities of Oxford and Cambridge along the River Thames.
Oxford won by two-and-three-quarter lengths in a time of 18 minutes 29 seconds, taking their overall lead in the competition to 37 – 30.
Document 6: The Boat Race is a side-by-side rowing competition between the boat clubs of University of Oxford and the University of Cambridge along the River Thames.
Oxford won by two-and-a-quarter lengths in a time of 20 minutes 50 seconds.
Document 7: The Boat Race is a side-by-side rowing race between crews from the Universities of Oxford and Cambridge along the River Thames.
Cambridge won by three-and-a-half lengths in a time of 18 minutes 15 seconds, the third-fastest winning time in history.

topic.name: Delaware State Highways
topic.description: The documents discuss various state highways in Delaware, detailing their routes, intersections, and historical context.

Document 1: Delaware Route 18 is a state highway located in Sussex County, Delaware.
DE 18 runs concurrent with DE 404 from its intersection with that highway southeast of Bridgeville to the eastern terminus.
Document 2: Delaware Route 42 is a state highway in Kent County, Delaware.
DE 42 passes through rural areas along with the towns of Kenton and Cheswold.
Document 3: Delaware Route 6 is a state highway in Kent County, Delaware.
The route passes through rural areas of northern Kent County as well as the towns of Smyrna and Clayton.
Document 4: Delaware Route 16 is an east-west state highway in Delaware, mainly across northern Sussex County.
The route runs from Maryland Route 16 at the Maryland border in Hickman to the Delaware Bay at Broadkill Beach.
Document 5: Delaware Route 300 is an 11.83-mile-long state highway in Kent County, Delaware.
The route is a continuation of Maryland Route 300 from the Maryland border near Everetts Corner.
Document 6: Delaware Route 26 is a state highway in Sussex County, Delaware.
The route passes through rural areas of southeastern Sussex County along with the communities of Dagsboro, Millville, Ocean View and Bethany Beach.

topic.name: Film Industry and Iconic Figures
topic.description: This cluster discusses prominent figures and studios in the film industry, highlighting their contributions and notable works.

Document 1: Clint Eastwood is an American actor, film director, producer, musician, and political figure.
Eastwood's greatest commercial successes have been the adventure comedy Every Which Way But Loose (1978) and its sequel.
Document 2: Walt Disney Animation Studios is an American animation studio that creates animated feature films, short films, and television specials for The Walt Disney Company.
The studio has produced 55 feature films, from Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs (1937) to Zootopia (2016).
Document 3: Vikram is an Indian film actor who predominantly appears in Tamil language films and has won seven Filmfare Awards.
Vikram's performance as a gravedigger with autism spectrum disorders in Bala's Pithamagan saw him win the National Film Award for Best Actor.
Document 4: Toy Story is a 1995 American computer-animated adventure buddy comedy film produced by Pixar Animation Studios.
Toy Story was the first feature-length computer-animated film and the first theatrical film produced by Pixar.
Document 5: Stanley Kubrick was an American film director, screenwriter, producer, cinematographer, editor, and photographer.
Part of the New Hollywood film-making wave, Kubrick's films are considered among the most important contributions to world cinema.
Document 6: Frozen is a 2013 American 3D computer-animated musical fantasy comedy film produced by Walt Disney Animation Studios.
Frozen tells the story of a fearless princess who sets off on an epic journey alongside a rugged iceman.
Document 7: Ranganathan Madhavan is an Indian actor, writer and film producer.
Madhavan has received two Filmfare Awards, an award from the Tamil Nadu State Film Awards alongside recognition and nominations from other organisations.

topic.name: Professional Wrestling History

topic.name: Philosophy and Literature
topic.description: The documents explore various philosophical concepts and their implications in literature, morality, and spirituality.

Document 1: Theory of Literature describes various aspects of literary theory, criticism, and history.
Wellek and Warren divide analysis of literature based on two approaches: extrinsic and intrinsic.
Document 2: Conscience is an aptitude, faculty, intuition or judgment that assists in distinguishing right from wrong.

The extent to which conscience informs moral judgment before an action and whether such moral judgments are or should be based in reason has occasioned debate through much of the history of Western philosophy.
Document 4: Alfred North Whitehead is best known as the defining figure of the philosophical school known as process philosophy.
He developed a comprehensive metaphysical system which radically departed from most of western philosophy.
Document 5: The teleological argument is an argument for the existence of God based on perceived evidence of deliberate design in the natural or physical world.
The teleological argument has a long association with the Abrahamic religions.
Document 6: Churches of Christ seek to base doctrine and practice on the Bible alone in order to be the church described in the New Testament.
The American Restoration Movement sought a return to the original teachings and practices of the New Testament.
Document 7: An ontological argument is a philosophical argument for the existence of God that uses ontology.
The first ontological argument in the Western Christian tradition was proposed by Anselm of Canterbury.

topic.name: Empowerment in Pop Music
topic.description: The documents discuss themes of empowerment and self-identity in pop music, particularly through the works of artists like Lady Gaga and Beyoncé.

1: the lyrics portray Gaga bidding farewell to her lovers
inspired by Gaga's love for her gay friends and admiration of gay love

the subjects of 'Best Thing I Never Had' are revenge and karma

the song was touted by Gaga as an experimental pop record

topic.name: Classic Video Games
topic.description: This topic encompasses iconic and influential video games and franchises that have shaped the gaming industry, particularly during the late 20th and early 21st centuries.

1: The Legend of Zelda: Twilight Princess is an action-adventure game developed and published by Nintendo.
Twilight Princess was considered the greatest entry in the Zelda series by many critics.
2: Another World is a 1991 cinematic platformer action-adventure game designed by Éric Chahi.
Another World was innovative in its use of cinematic effects in both real-time and cutscenes.
3: Super Mario Bros. is a platform video game developed and published by Nintendo.
The game has also sold enormously well, and was the best-selling game of all time for a single platform for approximately three decades.
4: Wonder Boy in Monster Land is an action role-playing platform video game developed by Westone Bit Entertainment.
Players control Wonder Boy through twelve linear levels as he makes his way through Monster Land.
5: The seventh generation includes consoles released since late 2005 by Nintendo, Microsoft, and Sony Computer Entertainment.
Video game consoles had become an important part of the global IT infrastructure.
6: Dragon Quest is the first role-playing video game in the Dragon Quest media franchise.
Dragon Quest has been ported and remade for several video game platforms.
7: Dragon Quest is a series of internationally best-selling console role-playing video game titles created by Yuji Horii.
The series has had a significant impact on the development of console RPGs.

topic.name: Tropical Cyclone Formation and Impact
topic.description: The documents discuss the formation, meteorological history, and impacts of various tropical cyclones and storms across different years and regions.

Document 1: Hurricane Isaac was the final tropical cyclone of the 2006 Atlantic hurricane season.
Effects from the storm were minimal and limited to gusty winds, rough surf, and sporadic rainfall.
Document 2: Hurricane Jimena was the tenth named storm and second hurricane of the 2003 Pacific hurricane season.
However, the storm weakened as it passed south of the islands and the effects from Jimena were minimal.
Document 3: Hurricane Dean was the fourth named storm and second hurricane of the 1989 Atlantic hurricane season.
The storm left $8.9 million (1989 USD, $17 million 2016 USD) and sixteen injuries across Bermuda, but no fatalities were reported.
Document 4: Tropical Storm Beryl made landfall just south of the United States – Mexico border in mid-August 2000, causing minimal damage.
One death was reported in Mexico due to drowning.
Document 5: Tropical Storm Becky was the second tropical cyclone and named storm of the 1970 Atlantic hurricane season.
Overall, damage was related to heavy rainfall that caused flooding in Tallahassee, Florida.
Document 6: Tropical Storm Alma of the 2008 Pacific hurricane season was the easternmost forming Pacific tropical cyclone on record.
In Costa Rica, heavy rainfall caused flooding and landslides, killing two and causing $35 million (USD) in damage.
Document 7: Hurricane Caroline was one of two tropical cyclones to affect northern Mexico during the 1975 Atlantic hurricane season.
There were only two deaths from Caroline, both indirect.

topic.name: College Football Teams and Seasons
topic.description: The documents discuss various college football teams, their seasons, key players, and significant games.

Document 1: The 1997 Michigan Wolverines football team represented the University of Michigan in the 1997 NCAA Division I-A football season.
Michigan compiled a perfect 12 – 0 record, won the Big Ten Conference championship.
Document 2: The 2010 Michigan Wolverines football team represented the University of Michigan in the 2010 NCAA Division I FBS football season.
Michigan became bowl eligible for the first time since 2007.
Document 3: The 1998 Music City Bowl was a postseason college football game between the Virginia Tech Hokies and Alabama Crimson Tide.
Virginia Tech's 8 – 3 record during the 1998 college football regular season was good enough to earn it a bid to the inaugural Music City Bowl game.
Document 4: The 1998 Gator Bowl was a post-season American college football bowl game between the Virginia Tech Hokies and the Tar Heels from the University of North Carolina.
The game ended in a 42-3 victory for North Carolina.
Document 5: The 2001 Gator Bowl was a post-season American college football bowl game between the Virginia Tech Hokies and the Clemson Tigers.
Virginia Tech entered the game headed by star quarterback Michael Vick.
Document 6: The 2012 Alabama Crimson Tide football team represented the University of Alabama in the 2012 National Collegiate Athletic Association (NCAA) Division I Football Bowl Subdivision (FBS) football season.

Document 7: The 2013 Penn State Nittany Lions football team represented Pennsylvania State University in the 2013 NCAA Division I FBS football season.
Penn State was ineligible to play in a bowl game for the 2013 season.

topic.name: Atlantic and Pacific hurricane seasons
topic.description: The documents discuss various Atlantic and Pacific hurricane seasons, detailing their activity, notable storms, and impacts over the years.

Document 1: 2000 Atlantic hurricane season was the first Atlantic hurricane season without a tropical cyclone in the month of July since 1993
the most significant storm of the season was Hurricane Keith, which caused extensive damage in Central America.
Document 2: 2004 Atlantic hurricane season was the costliest Atlantic hurricane season on record until surpassed by the following year
Hurricane Charley made landfall in Florida as a Category 4 hurricane causing $15.1 billion in damage in the United States alone.
Document 3: 1985 Pacific hurricane season is the third most active Pacific hurricane season on record
Hurricane Waldo caused moderate damage in Northwestern Mexico and one death in Kansas.
Document 4: 2012 Atlantic hurricane season was extremely active, tied with 1887, 1995, 2010, and 2011 for having the third-most named storms on record
Hurricane Sandy, which formed before Tony, became extratropical on October 29.
Document 5: 1990 Pacific hurricane season is the fifth most active season on record

Document 6: 1983 Pacific hurricane season was the longest season ever recorded at that time

Document 7: 1994 Pacific typhoon season was an extremely active season in the annual cycle of tropical cyclone formation in the Western North Pacific

Document 6: Stephen V was King of Hungary and Croatia between 1270 and 1272.
A civil war broke out between father and son.
